title: FINAL across Paritions fails to remove duplicates
description:
### Company or project name
An accounting crm platform.
### Describe what's wrong
When you have a ReplacingMergeTree table that uses a `PARITION BY` and a newly updated row is in a different partition then the `FINAL` doesn't remove the row in certain select statements that touch only the earlier partition.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/edea44e3-d1c6-463d-ab3d-97824c64515f
Tested in latest and 24.7.2.13
```sql
CREATE TABLE Transactions
(
`AccountID` Int64,
`ContributionID` Int64,
`ReceiptDate` Date,
`LastUpdatedOnUTC` DateTime64(3, 'UTC'),
)
ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(LastUpdatedOnUTC)
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(ReceiptDate)
PRIMARY KEY (AccountID, ContributionID)
ORDER BY (AccountID, ContributionID);
insert into Transactions VALUES (1,1234,'2025-10-01', '2025-10-01');
select 'OK-1',* from Transactions final where ContributionID = 1234;
insert into Transactions VALUES (1,1234,'2025-09-30', '2025-10-15');
select 'OK-2',* from Transactions final where ContributionID = 1234;
select 'BAD Date',* from Transactions final where ContributionID = 1234
AND ReceiptDate>='2025-10-01';
select 'BAD Date',* from (SELECT * FROM Transactions final WHERE ReceiptDate>='2025-09-30') as t where ContributionID = 1234
AND ReceiptDate>='2025-10-01';
```
### Expected behavior
The ReceiptDate of 9/30 should show for all the queries after when using the FINAL keyword.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE Transactions (AccountID BIGINT, ContributionID BIGINT, ReceiptDate DATE, LastUpdatedOnUTC DATETIME(3));
SELECT 'Check 1', * FROM Transactions WHERE ContributionID = 1234 ORDER BY LastUpdatedOnUTC DESC LIMIT 1;
SELECT 'Check 2', * FROM (SELECT * FROM Transactions WHERE ReceiptDate >= '2025-09-30' ORDER BY LastUpdatedOnUTC DESC LIMIT 1) AS t WHERE ContributionID = 1234 AND ReceiptDate >= '2025-10-01';
key_bug_features: FINAL fails to remove duplicates across partitions
